opp.ais_why:
Why the score is high:

Unlike many organizations that simply mention AI in press releases, First Gen has made AI part of its enterprise technology strategy.

Some notable indicators include:

A formal Digitalization Roadmap that specifically lists Data & AI as a strategic pillar.
Internal GenAI programs already being rolled out.
Investments in:
Hyperautomation
Robotics Process Automation (RPA)
Enterprise data pipelines
Advanced analytics
SAP roadmapping
AI governance and employee awareness
Increased investment in digitalization, cybersecurity, and automation year over year.

items.quote: Renato Castillo (VP & Chief Risk Officer, Executive Sponsor) absent from the current re-opening; no one on the call could commit budget
is it gonna be next year? Perhaps, if we get the budget approved for it
automation doesn't simply justify the cost; no ROI or savings figure articulated
we're also talking to another provider
Patrick has left; Soleil Yap named as replacement but unproven

metrics.narrative: The business problem is confirmed but never quantified. On the 1 July 2026 call the relevant stakeholder described procurement as 'mostly doing things manual' across a diverse Lopez Group conglomerate whose 'primary challenge' is aligning processes across diverse business units. Crucially the buyer said implementing a system to automate 'doesn't simply justify the cost of implementing that system for them', so they deployed smaller interim automation tools instead. No cost, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate or savings figure exists in any call or Salesforce field, and prior Next Steps (9 May 2024) show Finance and IT wanting an internal ROI discussion that was never quantified. Building a numeric S2P business case tied to their digitalization roadmap is the move that would convert the roadmap into a funded project.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Patrick Dela Rama; Soleil M Yap; Caroline E. De Lunas
champion.narrative: There is no active champion, and the prior one is gone. Patrick Dela Rama (Procurement Manager, Decision Maker, developing champion as of March 2024) 'is no longer part of the company', confirmed on the 1 July 2026 call. Soleil Yap is named as the person who 'replaced Patrick' but is unproven and has shown no advocacy behaviour. Caroline De Lunas remains in procurement but is not documented driving the deal. Napoleon Manaog (IT Officer) initiated the re-engagement and is the warmest current contact, but he sits in IT, not procurement, and his willingness to advocate for Zycus is untested. Securing a new procurement champion is the highest-order relationship move before any commercial step.

competition.narrative: The dominant current competitor is Do Nothing / status quo: the buyer has no approved budget, deployed interim automation tools, and framed this year as roadmap-only. A second, unnamed provider is also being consulted for 'industry guidance' (1 July 2026). Salesforce lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide and SAP Ariba; Coupa historically had an internal advocate in Vince Mabutas (coached by Coupa, August 2023), but the procurement team has since turned over, lowering that advocacy's current weight. Our wedge is agentic AI: the buyer is explicitly agentic-AI hungry and Zycus already embeds it (Anna/Merlin), plus strong Philippine conglomerate references (BDO and others) to counter status quo and the unnamed alternative.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is real but the buyer actively downplays its urgency. On 1 July 2026 they described largely manual procurement processes and, as a conglomerate, difficulty aligning 'diverse areas' onto a common route, calling process alignment 'one of their primary challenges.' However they also said automation alone 'doesn't simply justify the cost', and they have applied interim smaller automation tools to relieve pressure. The pain owner is procurement (Caroline De Lunas, Soleil Yap) sponsored through FPH IT. Confirmed qualitative pain, no quantified impact, and low stated urgency, which is why a compelling event and a numeric case are the levers to raise it.

paper_process.narrative: No current contracting mechanics exist. A mutual NDA was signed back in the 2023 engagement (Next Step 17-20 April 2023), but there is no active legal, MSA/SOW, InfoSec or procurement-channel activity in this re-opening, consistent with a roadmap-stage deal with no funded project. The forcing function is absent: no contract-expiration date and no committed budget. Visibility would be created by converting the roadmap into a funded project with a named procurement owner and a decision timeline, which is what the deal team should pursue before any paper motion is realistic.

economic_buyer.narrative: Renato Castillo (VP & Chief Risk Officer) is the Salesforce Executive Sponsor contact role and the standing budget-authority candidate, referenced historically as the sponsor/budget owner (Next Step 20 April 2023), but he has no engagement in the current re-opening and did not appear on the 1 July 2026 call. Right now the gate sits with the relevant stakeholder: the roadmap and any funding depend on 'all of the approvals needed, the budget at that', which the buyer said is contingent and likely next-year. So the EB is mapped by role but neither engaged nor bought in, and no one on the call could commit budget. Re-engaging Renato Castillo to test executive sponsorship and the budget path is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a roadmap-definition exercise, not a purchase, and IT is driving it. FPH IT (Grace as the business-to-IT link, Napoleon Manaog from solutions) are the current gateway, with a newly reconstituted procurement team ('a relatively young group who's really relying on IT') that includes Caroline De Lunas and Soleil Yap. The sequence the buyer described: gather thought leadership and an agentic-AI demo, shape a Source-to-Pay roadmap, then seek budget approval, with implementation only 'next year perhaps.' A second provider is being consulted in parallel. The approval chain and any procurement/committee sign-off are undefined for this new cycle and need to be re-mapped.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging, not formalized. On 1 July 2026 the buyer framed selection around fit to their enterprise-architecture roadmap ('Source-To-Pay is one of those work streams'), embedded/agentic AI across all enterprise solutions, and the ability to handle conglomerate complexity ('how your product line addresses such complexities'). They asked specifically whether Zycus has clients running agentic AI and wanted 'a generic industry example of how Zycus actually fits into a landscape.' There is no RFP or scored requirement set yet; these criteria are inferred from the discovery call and prior TOR/requirement-gathering history (Caroline's team shared a TOR draft in 2024). Confirming written criteria as the roadmap firms up is the next step.

cro_panel.header: First Gen Corporation — Qualified · $350K · closes 12 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 14 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); momentum 53 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 53 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 6 is anchored on the stage win (14), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 53, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

confidence_signals.summary: Re-warming after years dark: one fresh two-way call with a genuine buyer pull for thought leadership and a demo, but tempered by no budget, next-year timing, a departed champion, and a parallel provider.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 14 April 2023, stage Qualified (re-qualified 26 June 2026 from Initial Interest); no validation-stage submission dates populated. Close date 12 December 2026 is ~162 days out. On the 1 July 2026 call the buyer stated implementation would be 'next year perhaps, if we get the budget approved' and that this year's goal is only a roadmap. A realistic path (roadmap and thought-leadership, agentic-AI demo, budget approval, RFP issue and response, shortlist and use-case validation, commercials, legal/InfoSec, contracting) cannot complete inside 162 days when the buyer has not funded the project. The deal is genuinely re-engaged and healthy for its early stage, so the sole real problem is that the date will slip well into 2027.
evidence: Avoma intro call 1 July 2026: FPH IT (Grace, business relationship management) and Napoleon Manaog (IT solutions) re-opened the conversation; two-way, positive, exploratory
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'Will it be implemented this year? No.' and 'is it gonna be next year? Perhaps, if we get the budget approved for it' (no funded project)
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'we're also talking to another provider ... we are still in the process of getting industry guidance' (parallel evaluation, roadmap-stage)
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'Patrick is no longer part of the company' - the developing champion has left; 'a relatively young group who's really relying on IT'
Field history 26 June 2026: StageName Initial Interest -> Qualified and Amount 500000 -> 350000 (by Carl Kimball)
Next Step 26 June 2026: Napoleon Manaog outreach to kick-start an eval; Carl to set up online discussion and F2F in Manila
AIS 8.8 Strong corroborated on the call: buyer wants enterprise-wide agentic AI ('we're trying to have it in all of the enterprise solutions')
Contact Role Renato Castillo (VP & Chief Risk Officer, Executive Sponsor) - no documented engagement in the recent cycle; budget authority not exercised
north_star_verdict.headline: After a multi-year stall, First Gen re-engaged on a two-way intro call 1 July 2026 (FPH IT plus Napoleon Manaog), but they are only building a Source-to-Pay roadmap this year with no approved budget, implementation 'next year perhaps', and one other provider also in the mix. The relationship is live and warm again, yet the 12 December 2026 close date is fiction: the buyer explicitly said any purchase depends on next-year budget approval.

flags: Champion gone: Patrick Dela Rama left; secure Soleil Yap or Caroline De Lunas as a procurement champion before investing in demos, or the roadmap engagement stalls again.
Beat Do Nothing: the buyer says automation does not justify the cost, so land a quantified S2P ROI case tied to their digitalization roadmap to convert roadmap into a funded 2027 project.
Lead with agentic AI: AIS is 8.8 and the buyer wants enterprise-wide agentic AI, so make Anna/Merlin and local references the wedge versus the unnamed alternative provider.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal reset when its champion left; the win path is to rebuild a procurement champion, quantify the S2P case to beat status quo, and lead with agentic AI as the differentiator.

customer_preference.evidence: 'we're also talking to another provider ... we are still in the process of getting industry guidance' - neutral, roadmap-stage, no Zycus lean on the 1 July 2026 call.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is Do Nothing / status quo: on 1 July 2026 the buyer said automation 'doesn't simply justify the cost', has no approved budget, and set implementation to 'next year perhaps.' A second unnamed provider is being consulted in parallel for roadmap guidance (medium, active). Coupa, GEP Worldwide and SAP Ariba remain on the Salesforce field; Coupa's historic internal advocate Vince Mabutas (coached August 2023) carries less current weight now that procurement has turned over. Certinal was a peripheral 2023 mention and has faded. No competitor is buyer-preferred today.

items.grounding_quote: We do have a rough documentation of the process. How do we do this? Do we submit it to you guys?

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 8.8 Strong is strongly corroborated by the call: the buyer explicitly wants agentic AI across all enterprise solutions and asked which clients run it. Zycus positioned Anna (autonomous negotiating agent) and tail-spend savings well. Positioning is strong for the stage; the task is to keep Merlin/Anna the centrepiece of the roadmap engagement.

items.quote: We're trying to have it in all of the enterprise solutions that we have
items.criterion: Enterprise-wide agentic AI across all solutions

items.quote: we are a conglomerate ... they have to align somehow, and right now that's one of their primary challenges
items.criterion: Handle conglomerate complexity and align diverse business-unit processes (Lopez Group)

items.quote: We'd appreciate any help we could get on thought leadership
items.criterion: Industry thought leadership and roadmap guidance for Source-to-Pay

items.quote: implementing a system to automate their processes doesn't simply justify the cost

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on agentic AI and conglomerate-scale process fit, but exposed on cost justification and on Zycus's reluctance to invest before a committed project.

hard.ais_why:
Why the score is high:

Unlike many organizations that simply mention AI in press releases, First Gen has made AI part of its enterprise technology strategy.

Some notable indicators include:

A formal Digitalization Roadmap that specifically lists Data & AI as a strategic pillar.
Internal GenAI programs already being rolled out.
Investments in:
Hyperautomation
Robotics Process Automation (RPA)
Enterprise data pipelines
Advanced analytics
SAP roadmapping
AI governance and employee awareness
Increased investment in digitalization, cybersecurity, and automation year over year.
